name: Ben Tucker
abstract: Benjamin M. Tucker (December 13, 1930 – June 4, 2013) was an American jazz bassist who appeared on hundreds of recordings.

concerts.label: WLIB: 12th Annual Festival of Music and Drama

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I went back to Japan a little later in that year with a special drum tour that had Art Blakey, Elvin Jones, Tony Williams, McCoy Tyner, Ben Tucker played bass, Wayne Shorter and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I went again later on in 1966 with a drum tour. It was Tony Williams, Elvin Jones, Art Blakey, McCoy Tyner, Ben Tucker on bass, Wayne Shorter and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Well when we went to Japan on one of George Wein’s tours. This was supposed to be like the drum battle so it was Art Blakey, Tony Williams and myself. Oh yeah Wayne Shorter, Ben Tucker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: And Ben Tucker and Lex Humphries. I didn't play. ... I just went to observe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
